meta.title: Gear — WKND Adventures
meta.description: Tested and verified gear recommendations for outdoor adventures.

image.alt: Gear laid out for a backcountry trip

hero.heading: Gear
hero.lead: What goes in the pack matters. No affiliate links, no brand deals. Just what we actually use.

sections.heading: Buy once. Pack light. Leave nothing.
sections.body: Three rules. Every guide, every review, every gear list on this site follows them without exception.

sections.heading: Sub-10 lb: What to Cut, What to Keep
sections.description: Taylor spent three years cutting weight from a 22 lb base down to 9.4. The answer wasn't expensive gear — it was hard questions. What do you actually need? What are you carrying out of habit? This guide is the honest breakdown of every decision.

image.alt: Ultralight backpacking kit laid out on a tarp

sections.heading: What Goes In
sections.body: Tested across multiple summer alpine routes in the Mont Blanc Massif and Écrins. Assumes mountain hut accommodation is unavailable. Base weight: 9.2 kg. Tested in the Saharan margin of southern Morocco and the Wadi Rum, Jordan. Assumes no water resupply for 48-hour stretches. Base weight: 7.8 kg.

items.title: Buy once
items.text: Good gear outlasts trends. Choose carefully, use forever.

items.title: Pack light
items.text: Every gram is a decision you'll revisit on mile 20.

sections.heading: Gear is only half the story.
sections.body: Read the adventures where it gets used.
